# automaticstay.org

> Plain-English guide to the automatic stay under 11 U.S.C. Section 362.

automaticstay.org is a free, open-source educational resource about the automatic stay in bankruptcy. Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network.

## Pages

- https://automaticstay.org/: Overview of the automatic stay -- your shield against creditors
- https://automaticstay.org/what-it-covers.html: What the automatic stay covers -- lawsuits, garnishments, foreclosures, repossessions, collection calls, utility shutoffs
- https://automaticstay.org/violations.html: When creditors violate the stay -- 362(k) damages, what to do, common violations
- https://automaticstay.org/exceptions.html: Exceptions to the stay -- criminal proceedings, domestic support, tax audits, police power
- https://automaticstay.org/serial-filer-limits.html: Repeat filer limits -- 362(c)(3) 30-day stay, 362(c)(4) no stay
- https://automaticstay.org/motion-for-relief.html: Motion for relief from stay -- when creditors ask to lift the stay
- https://automaticstay.org/how-long-does-it-last.html: How long the automatic stay lasts in Chapter 7 vs Chapter 13
- https://automaticstay.org/car-repossessed.html: What to do if your car was repossessed before or after filing
- https://automaticstay.org/creditor-still-calling.html: What to do when a creditor keeps calling after you file bankruptcy
- https://automaticstay.org/index-es.html: Guia en espanol -- La suspension automatica

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
